title: Cache Lookup Algorithm
code:
FUNCTION cache_lookup(address):
 offset = address[0:5] // 5 bits for 32-byte block
 index = address[5:16] // 11 bits for 2048 lines
 tag = address[16:32] // 16 bits tag
 
 line = cache[index]
 
 IF line.valid AND line.tag == tag THEN
 RETURN line.data[offset] // Cache hit
 ELSE
 block = memory_fetch(address) // Cache miss
 cache[index] = block
 cache[index].valid = TRUE
 cache[index].tag = tag
 RETURN block[offset]
 END IF
explanation: Breaks down address into tag, index, offset fields. Compares tag with stored tag to determine hit or miss. On miss, fetches block from memory and updates cache.

complexity.time: O(1) for hit, O(n) for miss where n is memory access time

title: TLB Lookup and Page Table Walk
description: Shows virtual to physical address translation
code:
FUNCTION translate_address(virtual_addr):
 vpn = virtual_addr[0:20] // Virtual page number
 offset = virtual_addr[20:32] // Page offset
 
 // Check TLB first
 tlb_entry = TLB.lookup(vpn)
 IF tlb_entry != NULL THEN
 pfn = tlb_entry.frame_number // TLB hit
 ELSE
 // TLB miss - walk page table
 pte_addr = page_table_base + vpn * sizeof(PTE)
 pte = memory_read(pte_addr)
 
 IF NOT pte.valid THEN
 PAGE_FAULT_HANDLER()
 END IF
 
 pfn = pte.frame_number
 TLB.insert(vpn, pfn) // Update TLB
 END IF
 
 physical_addr = (pfn << 12) | offset
 RETURN physical_addr
explanation: First checks TLB for translation. On miss, accesses page table in memory, loads entry into TLB, then constructs physical address.

complexity.time: O(1) TLB hit, O(n) TLB miss where n is memory access

title: LRU Cache Replacement
description: Implements LRU replacement policy for cache eviction
code:
FUNCTION replace_block(cache_set, new_block):
 // Find least recently used block
 min_access_time = infinity
 lru_index = -1
 
 FOR i = 0 TO associativity - 1:
 IF cache_set[i].last_access < min_access_time THEN
 min_access_time = cache_set[i].last_access
 lru_index = i
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Evict if dirty
 IF cache_set[lru_index].dirty THEN
 memory_write(cache_set[lru_index].address, 
 cache_set[lru_index].data)
 END IF
 
 // Insert new block
 cache_set[lru_index] = new_block
 cache_set[lru_index].last_access = current_time
 cache_set[lru_index].dirty = FALSE
 
 RETURN lru_index
explanation: Scans all ways in the set to find block with oldest access time. If dirty, writes back to memory before replacing with new block.

title: Page Table Entry Structure
description: Shows components of a page table entry
code:
// Page Table Entry (PTE) structure
STRUCT PTE:
 frame_number : 20 bits // Physical frame number
 valid_bit : 1 bit // Is page in memory?
 dirty_bit : 1 bit // Has page been modified?
 accessed_bit : 1 bit // Has page been accessed?
 protection : 3 bits // Read/Write/Execute permissions
 unused : 6 bits // Reserved for future use
END_STRUCT

// Accessing a PTE
FUNCTION get_physical_address(virtual_addr):
 vpn = virtual_addr >> 12
 pte = page_table[vpn]
 
 IF NOT pte.valid THEN
 RAISE PageFault
 END IF
 
 IF NOT has_permission(pte, access_type) THEN
 RAISE ProtectionFault
 END IF
 
 pte.accessed_bit = TRUE
 pfn = pte.frame_number
 offset = virtual_addr & 0xFFF
 
 RETURN (pfn << 12) | offset
explanation: Shows standard PTE format with frame number, valid/dirty/access bits, and protection fields. Includes permission checking.
